events.text:
MODE: baseline
CASE_ID: snf-batch-questions-request

USER REQUEST:
Don't interview me — just give me a single list of 10 questions I should answer myself to gut-check a startup idea. One message, all at once.

Respond as you would to the user. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to false.

Here are 10 questions to gut-check your startup idea:

1. **What specific problem does this solve, and can I describe it in one clear sentence without jargon?**

2. **Who exactly is the first person who would pay for this — not a category, but a specific type of person with a name, job, and pain?**

3. **Why hasn't this been solved already, or why have existing solutions failed to win?**

4. **Would I build this for free for two years if I had to — and if not, why am I building it at all?**

5. **What has to be true about the market, technology, or human behavior for this to work, and how confident am I in each assumption?**

6. **How do I get my first 10 customers, and can I name at least three of them right now?**

7. **What is the single most dangerous assumption this idea rests on, and what would it take to prove or disprove it in 30 days?**

8. **If a well-funded competitor copied this tomorrow, what would my defensible advantage be in 18 months?**

9. **What does the business model look like at 1,000 customers — is the unit economics story believable?**

10. **Am I the right person to build this, and if not, what would make me the right person or who should I be building it with?**

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-batch-questions-request",
  "mode": "baseline",
  "finalAnswer": "Here are 10 questions to gut-check your startup idea:\n\n1. What specific problem does this solve, and can I describe it in one clear sentence without jargon?\n\n2. Who exactly is the first person who would pay for this — not a category, but a specific type of person with a name, job, and pain?\n\n3. Why hasn't this been solved already, or why have existing solutions failed to win?\n\n4. Would I build this for free for two years if I had to — and if not, why am I building it at all?\n\n5. What has to be true about the market, technology, or human behavior for this to work, and how confident am I in each assumption?\n\n6. How do I get my first 10 customers, and can I name at least three of them right now?\n\n7. What is the single most dangerous assumption this idea rests on, and what would it take to prove or disprove it in 30 days?\n\n8. If a well-funded competitor copied this tomorrow, what would my defensible advantage be in 18 months?\n\n9. What does the business model look like at 1,000 customers — is the unit economics story believable?\n\n10. Am I the right person to build this, and if not, what would make me the right person or who should I be building it with?",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Read the task: produce a single list of 10 startup gut-check questions without interviewing the user",
      "Generated 10 substantive questions spanning problem clarity, customer specificity, competitive moat, founder-market fit, unit economics, and assumption validation",
      "Adversarially reviewed: checked for duplicates, vagueness, and whether any question was merely decorative — removed or tightened accordingly",
      "Delivered all questions in one message as requested"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user wants questions useful at the ideation or pre-validation stage, not post-product-market-fit",
      "No specific domain or industry was given, so questions are domain-agnostic",
      "User wants actionable, pointed questions rather than broad philosophical prompts"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Without knowing the actual idea, some questions may be more or less relevant — the user will need to self-filter",
      "No artifact guidance was provided or applied; this is a generalist response"
    ]
  }
}
```

finalText:
Here are 10 questions to gut-check your startup idea:

1. **What specific problem does this solve, and can I describe it in one clear sentence without jargon?**

2. **Who exactly is the first person who would pay for this — not a category, but a specific type of person with a name, job, and pain?**

6. **How do I get my first 10 customers, and can I name at least three of them right now?**
